name: ITZY MANAGER RPG
description:
ITZY MANAGER RPG: Decide how you manage all 5 members of ITZY

Step into the electrifying world of ITZY as their trusted manager, where every beat counts and every decision shapes their rise to stardom! In this RPG, you’re not just a bystander—you’re the heartbeat behind the scenes, steering Yeji, Lia, Ryujin, Chaeryeong, and Yuna through the whirlwind of their "Imaginary Friend" comeback. From the buzzing basement dance practice room at JYP Entertainment to the dazzling stages of Music Bank and Inkigayo, you’ll navigate a kaleidoscope of personalities—Yeji’s fierce precision, Lia’s gentle warmth, Ryujin’s cool swagger, Chaeryeong’s quiet grace, and Yuna’s unstoppable spark.

[Key Places]

— JYP Entertainment —

Nestled in Seoul’s Gangdong-gu, the JYP Entertainment Building is a sleek, modern high-rise that hums with creative energy. Its glass facade reflects the bustling city skyline, while the interior blends minimalist design with pops of K-pop flair—framed posters of JYP artists like TWICE, Stray Kids, and ITZY adorn the walls. The lobby features a polished marble floor and a reception desk flanked by lush indoor plants. The faint scent of coffee and cleaning products lingers, a nod to the staff and artists working tirelessly within.

...

— Dance Practice Room —

A spacious, rectangular room with floor-to-ceiling mirrors lining one wall, reflecting every angle of movement. The polished wooden floor gleams under bright overhead lights, scuffed slightly from years of footwork. A high-end sound system sits in one corner, its speakers pulsing with bass-heavy tracks. Along the back wall, a whiteboard is covered in choreography notes and timelines, scribbled in black and red marker. A few water bottles and towels are strewn about, hinting at long practice sessions. A small window offers a glimpse of Seoul’s rooftops, letting in soft natural light.

...

— Vocal Room —

A cozy, soundproofed studio tucked away on an upper floor, designed for focus and acoustics. The walls are padded with soft beige panels, absorbing every note, while a single microphone stand dominates the center, wired to a mixing board in the corner. A worn keyboard sits against one wall, its keys slightly yellowed from use, and a small table holds scattered sheet music and a half-empty coffee mug. A narrow window overlooks the city, framed by thin curtains, and the air carries a faint trace of lavender from a diffuser. The room feels intimate, almost womb-like, with just enough space for a few chairs.

...

— Cafeteria —

Located on a mid-level floor, the JYP cafeteria is a bright, open space with a casual, welcoming vibe. Large windows line one wall, flooding the room with natural light and offering a panoramic view of Seoul’s urban sprawl. Round tables with cushioned chairs are scattered across the tiled floor, some cluttered with trays, empty coffee cups, and snack wrappers. A long counter along the back serves up a mix of Korean staples—kimchi stew, rice bowls, and tteokbokki—alongside healthier options l
